topic: How to further improve the review logic in sk-deep-research and related commands/agents

focus: Q1 canonical source-of-truth mapping for review mode definitions and propagation
noveltyJustification: This iteration converted a known general concern about drift into a concrete source map, identified the main drift mechanisms, and added a repo-native generation pattern that was not in the prior context summary.

focus: Q1 continuation - canonical review-mode contract manifest and propagation workflow
noveltyJustification: This iteration converted the manifest recommendation into an exact schema, ownership matrix, render-routing contract, and validator design, plus added repo-native generated-block and verify-mode precedents.

This iteration converted the complexity concern into a concrete reduced operator model, showed where current categories overlap, and anchored the simplification against current official review-tool patterns rather than repo-only intuition.

focus: Q3 convergence threshold validation via replay of review runs 012/013 and archived v1 proposals
noveltyJustification: This iteration turned unvalidated convergence proposals into a concrete replay method and exposed a specific regression: after the 7-to-4 dimension collapse, the unchanged algorithm stops one iteration early on spec 012 unless coverage stop is delayed and stuck/no-progress is decoupled from rolling convergence.

focus: Q4 cross-reference verification redesign: machine-verifiable traceability checks, storage, and convergence policy
noveltyJustification: This iteration converted appendix-level cross-reference prose into a concrete contract design with stable protocol IDs, core-vs-overlay applicability, typed JSONL storage, and explicit convergence semantics.

focus: Q5 review report quality: planner-ready remediation packet and machine-readable handoff for /spec_kit:plan
noveltyJustification: This iteration converted a general report-quality concern into a concrete handoff design by mapping review output directly onto spec/plan template fields and adding a single canonical planning-packet schema for parser use.

focus: Q6 @deep-review agent architecture: permission boundaries, tool budget, dispatch contract, adversarial validation, and cross-runtime parity
noveltyJustification: This iteration turned a broad agent-architecture question into a concrete operating model: scratch-only write hardening, profile-based budgets, structured dispatch state, typed P0/P1 claim adjudication, and front-matter parity checks across runtimes.

This iteration added a concrete integration boundary, surfaced baseline drift between the quick reference and agent docs, and identified shared review-core contracts plus orchestrator-mediated packet exchange as the safe reuse path instead of nested agent calls.
